reviews.review_title: Movement around the table not good
reviews.review_description: the game should have this option  :  when holding down a button you should be avable to move the table   back and and forth,left and right just by moving the q3 controlers    and anothe for up and down   ...........This is the way ...... for natural movement

developer_response.body: Hi, thank you for your updated feedback. I have responded to multiple previous updates of this review, please be assured that I have taken your suggestions regarding movement controls and hand tracking into consideration for future updates.

reviews.review_description:
MR support is nice for those that requested it & it seems to work as intended so a nice addition, i feel the pockets are still too tight (speaking about snooker) and the pockets are still sometimes glitching ie accepting pots in odd semi graphical glitch ways (think of a matrix visual glitch, a ball entering the pocket at a speed or + angle the pocket doesn't understand therefore the ball goes in but the pocket itself has a ever so slight visual glitch) if you've played it you'll no what im referring to there

The audio of the balls being split/potted is basic but okay, still needs more variety in audio, a Crisp long pot hit to Perfection has a very unique sound, as does a Perfectly hit thin snip or safety shot nd the audio hasn't got these yet, its lacking the variety needed, 

I think its okay but certainly not great (ironically better the the competition which is awful) however i will say this, it suffers from tracking issues, a cueing sport at its core its a matter is fractions, tiny tiny adjustments to ya aim & stroke power so to suffer tracking issues is a big big deal, the Quest headsets have limitations but its also on the devs to adjust and offer the best solutionsto those limitations, VR Boxing games suffer similar tracking issues,

In the area of tracking, this still needs refinement, maybe thats impossible, i can't say but the game is decently enjoyable when the tracking works full time, i'd enjoy it alot more if there was less tracking dusruptions when i really am trying to make tiny adjustments to my aim (ya can't play competitively with these issues) and the pockets are really annoying at times, they are worse then (unforgiving) most of the time) and then other times they glitch and accept pots but its a visual mess, long pots are seemingly easier to calculate & take then any close shot, + if you have a close shot anywhere even remotely close to the cushion that requires a little side/backspin to get off of the cushion at an angle, the pockets will not accept it (I've potted long balls with a touch of spin on em to bend then straighten the white out before contact then the pot) I've no issue with sidespin i no what im doing, its not a lack of skill, 

There is an issue with those shots, red not touching but close to the cushion & pocket of which you need back+side to pot & get out of there, the pocket will deny you those shots 95% of the time, its the games issue 

Not to be too negetive, the games still enjoyable (especially if you have the aiming aid on) from what i remember regardless of side/spin the ball will go exactly where that line says it will go, Perfect for those that wana pickup & play) the game is really close to being as good as having a real table (minus the hassle that can come with) 

My last real issue is regardless of cueing settings ive chose (realistic settings is obv my style) the cue can still go through the sides/cushions, that can sorta be fixed via table realignment i guess but its better to have a in game option to properly fix that, the side of the table/cushion it all needs to be 100% solid so your hand/cue does not go through it at all (that'll also go a ways to help improve aiming aswel, i havent found anyone to play online yet but agen until my concerns are met/fixed i don't particularly want to because it'll frustrate me too much

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the detailed feedback, appreciate you updating the review!

On the snooker side, pockets are still something I’m actively refining. The near-cushion/spin shots you mentioned are really useful examples, and the occasional visual glitch on pots is on my list to clean up too.

Agree on the audio too, I'm definitely looking to add more variation.

On the cue, it currently can pass through the cushions by design, but I agree having a proper physical limit there would help realism and it’s something I want to add.

Really helpful breakdown overall, this kind of feedback is great for guiding improvements 👍

reviews.review_title: So many improvements, love English pool.
reviews.review_description: I enjoyed this game. Had to use teleporting to move, but that works quite well. I'd love to be able to spin the table around the cue ball or the blue spot. The English pool table is nice, the balls seem more in proportion than with a rival game. The small snooker table is nice. I would love to play on the large snooker table, but I need more movement options.

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for updating your review, I really appreciate it. Your support and feedback has genuinely helped, so it means a lot to see you come back and update the rating. More improvements are on the way too, thanks for sticking with Backspin!

reviews.review_description:
I was looking forward to this game, and I know it's only out but if it's not going to work with the pro controllers how can it be considered a poo/snooker sim. Let's hope the dev patches it quickly, I play most of the other pool/snooker games (bhp, MP, Unlimited snooker etc) work with the pro controllers out of the box.
Very disappointed ☹️
I will of course change my rating if the dev fixes the game.
EDIT:- the dev has now pushed a fix for the pro controllers in double quick time which is very hopeful for the game going forward and menus are all working but if ever a game needed to be tested before general release this was it, I do feel the gfx are not too bad but the game mechanics in my view are very clunky at best, aiming is pretty tough, when I played the ai, each time I placed my bridge hand on the table it was at a different height and when I tried to use the rest, sometimes I couldn't hit the ball, I have only played the Chinese 8 ball so far so I can't comment on all the games but the 8 ball table I played on in my view had decent dimensions but the cushions need a lot of work and the pockets are tight, I'm upping my rating to 3 stars mainly because of how quickly the dev addressed the issues with the pro controllers.
I will keep the game because I do think if the Dev puts in the work this could be a decent game, but it's going to take a lot of work.

developer_response.body: Thanks again for taking the time to share such detailed feedback and for sticking with it. Since launch I’ve been actively prioritising cueing stability, bridge consistency, rest usability and high-speed collision reliability, as those have clearly been the biggest pain points. The next update focuses heavily on tightening up those fundamentals, and I’m continuing to iterate quickly based on player reports. I really appreciate the patience, this kind of feedback genuinely helps shape the game!

reviews.review_description:
Great game, but it feels like it lags a little at times. It’s not critical though, and overall I definitely recommend buying it. Right now, I think this is one of the best billiards games available. I really hope the developers won’t abandon the game.
I’d love to see more people buy it so it becomes easier to find opponents in online matches. I’m giving it 5 stars — it’s awesome.

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for the review and kind words 🙂 Really glad you’re enjoying the game! I appreciate the note about the occasional lag too, the upcoming patch contains some changes that should directly help with that.

reviews.review_description:
EDIT: Scale has been fixed (sort of). In English pool the cue ball is 48mm diameter, which you now have fixed.. Thank you! but the red and yellow balls should be 51mm diameter, which in physics of real English pool makes the cueball also lighter in weight therefor having more control on stun shots and backspin. I'm feeling too much followthrough on the cueball when it should be stunned. Would also like to note that bank shots and the rebound off a cushion atm seem to be adding unapplied sidespin, exaggerating the angle played. Try doubling the cueball from baulk to corner pocket to see what i mean. Really impressed with all the improvements you've made so far though. No other game has done English pool correct since Virtual Pool 64 on the N64 and i want your game to be my go-to game for pool. Keep up the great work!


PREVIOUS REVIEW:

The scale of the table and ball sizes are well off on English pool, the table's too small and also the balls, it's like playing marbles on a coffee table. This makes it hard to be accurate as the contact point is miniscule. Snooker balls seem too small also. I really hope the scale can be fixed soon as i believe the physics will improve once the scaling is correct. Will update the rating if it gets fixed.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the detailed follow-up, much appreciated! You’re right that doubles and cushion rebounds really highlight the remaining physics issues, and that’s exactly what the next physics-focused update will be targeting. Glad the scaling changes are already helping, and thanks for sticking with it!

reviews.review_title: Game is getting better all the time!
reviews.review_description:
After a shakyish start this dev has really put in the work to update regularly and address users feedback. As a consequence the game is really starting to shine. Just tonight there were big improvements to the visuals and sounds increasing realism and immersion.

If the dev keeps updating as he has been then this game will soon be in a really good place and no doubt gain more fans.

developer_response.body: Thanks a lot for the kind words, really appreciate it. Glad the recent updates are landing well. I’ll keep the improvements coming!

reviews.review_description:
Edit: Lots of improvements recently and devs take the time to respond to reviews.
 Only played snooker so far. 60 second time limit on shots is a stupid idea... kept running out of time to play a shot because it's so difficult to even move around the table, never mind lining up a shot which takes forever too.
As expected hardly any snooker rules have been incorporated- no free ball, give 4 points away after accidently hitting the black etc. Cpu player's cue protrudes through the cushion. Can't pick up white ball off the floor. The Rest (cross/spider) stays on the table even after my turn has finished.
And ai opponents are laughably bad. Even Pro opponent couldn't hit a cows backside with a rusty banjo.
Still, I'll probably give this game 5 stars when it's finished and working properly,

developer_response.body: Thanks a lot for taking another look! I’m glad the recent updates have landed well for you.

reviews.review_description:
I agree with previous reviews, this should of been a "early access" release,  £15 is far too much for its current state of development. 

Everything about the play feels unnatural, even the rotation and movement around the table is bad. Coming from miracle pool no hand snapping to table really hurts it. I would not recommend purchasing this until there is a lot of work done on it.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the feedback. Movement and table navigation were weak points at launch.

Since this review, several updates have focused specifically on that: smoother movement around the table, adjustable movement and turn speed sliders (with the option to disable the VR environment for comfort), and an optional experimental bridge-hand clamping feature to anchor the front hand to the table.

These changes have made lining up shots feel far more natural, especially for players coming from other VR cue sports titles.

reviews.review_description:
The developer has been working hard updating issues daily, for that reason 5 stars,
Also the game now I can actually play sitting down is pretty good, graphics are nice and plays well, not perfect and the game seems to be getting plenty of updates so fingers crossed the game  will get better over time, the game does not deserve a 1 star I think that is a little harsh, it’s not the best pool game for physics yet but it is better than most of the others

developer_response.body: Thanks a lot! I really appreciate the support and the thoughtful review. Glad the recent updates have helped.

reviews.review_description: Would be a 5 star if I could use my own cue

developer_response.body: Thanks for the review, really appreciate it! Using your own cue is a great shout and something I’m keen to look into soon.

reviews.review_description: By the time this review appears, there should be a full (unscripted and decidedly unprofessional) review of this game on my channel.  I have spent the last three days playing nothing else but this game, as full size snooker in VR has always been a dream of mine and now it is finally here... Yes there are still issues (some of which I showcase in my video), which when addressed will push this game closer to its five star potential (the current rating of 3.5 is about right, but I've given it 4 stars, because I can see what it can become).  The bridging hand needs work, but is more than functional as it stands, there are some issues within snooker that are curious, such as illegally potted colours not being respotted (happened twice in around 15 games), ongoing breaks suddenly stopping and the control being handed to the other player/ai (which has happened around 5 times), but I am positive that the developer will actively work towards solving these issues and continually improve what is already a tremendous amount of fun.  Having a full-size snooker table in my kitchen and being able to play against the charmingly aggressive ai player is a constant joy.  I have quite a lot of recorded footage from the last three days that I will willingly share if the developer would like to view it. Thank you for making this available and I look forward to continued improvements.

developer_response.body:
Thank you very much for the review and for taking the time to make such a detailed video. I really appreciate the support.

You are absolutely right about the snooker specific issues you highlighted, particularly around respotting and breaks ending incorrectly. These are known problems in vs AI mode and are being actively worked on.

I am really glad you are enjoying the full size snooker experience in VR and the AI matches. Feedback like this helps guide priorities, and more improvements are on the way. Thanks again for taking the time to play and share your thoughts.
